findings.title: Migrate from Apache Kafka 2.x to Kafka 3.7+
findings.description: Migrate the core message broker from Apache Kafka 2.8 to Kafka 3.7+ to eliminate vulnerabilities and ensure compatibility with the KRaft consensus protocol.
assumptions: All producer and consumer services can be updated without significant downtime.

evidence.excerpt: Core message broker runs Kafka 2.8 which reaches end-of-life in Q1 2026.

recommendations: Plan migration strategy and timeline for all services.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the current Kafka version is 2.8 WHEN the migration to Kafka 3.7+ is initiated THEN all services should function without errors and maintain performance metrics.

findings.title: Evaluate Alternatives to VendorX's ETL SDK
findings.description: Assess open-source alternatives to VendorX's proprietary ETL SDK to mitigate vendor lock-in and prepare for potential price increases.
assumptions: Open-source alternatives will meet performance requirements.

evidence.excerpt: The data transformation layer relies on VendorX's proprietary ETL SDK.

recommendations: Conduct a comparative analysis of potential alternatives.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the current reliance on VendorX's ETL SDK WHEN alternatives are evaluated THEN a report should be generated detailing the pros and cons of each option.

findings.title: Upgrade Python from 3.9 to 3.12
findings.description: Migrate all pipeline worker services from Python 3.9 to Python 3.12 to ensure continued support and security.
assumptions: All dependencies will be compatible with Python 3.12.

evidence.excerpt: Pipeline worker services run Python 3.9 which reaches end-of-life October 2025.

recommendations: Identify and resolve dependency incompatibilities before migration.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the current Python version is 3.9 WHEN the upgrade to 3.12 is performed THEN all services should operate without errors and pass all unit tests.

findings.title: Create Dependency Health Dashboard
findings.description: Develop a centralized dashboard to monitor the health of dependencies, including EOL dates and known CVEs, to facilitate proactive management.
assumptions: The dashboard will integrate with existing tools like GitHub Dependabot.

evidence.excerpt: There is no centralized view of dependency health across the platform.

recommendations: Design the dashboard interface with user feedback in mind.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the dashboard is implemented WHEN a user accesses it THEN they should see a list of all dependencies with their health status and upgrade paths.

findings.title: Implement Automated CVE Alerting
findings.description: Integrate automated alerting for known CVEs into the dependency health dashboard to ensure timely remediation of vulnerabilities.
assumptions: The alerting system will be reliable and timely.

evidence.excerpt: Build a dashboard showing all runtime dependencies with versions, EOL dates, known CVEs.

recommendations: Test the alerting system with various CVE scenarios.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a CVE is reported WHEN the alerting system is triggered THEN the relevant team should receive a notification within 5 minutes.

findings.title: Monitor Events Processed Per Day
findings.description: Establish a monitoring system for the North Star metric: total events processed per day to gauge platform performance.
assumptions: The monitoring system will accurately reflect real-time data.

evidence.excerpt: The North Star metric for DataPipe is the total number of events processed per day.

recommendations: Create visualizations for the metric to enhance understanding.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the monitoring system is in place WHEN events are processed THEN the total should be accurately reflected in the dashboard within 1 minute.

findings.title: Track Services on EOL Dependencies
findings.description: Implement tracking for the number of services relying on end-of-life dependencies to assess risk levels.
assumptions: Tracking will be automated and updated regularly.

evidence.excerpt: This primary KPI tracks the number of services relying on end-of-life dependencies.

recommendations: Integrate tracking with the dependency health dashboard.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the tracking system is implemented WHEN a service is identified as using an EOL dependency THEN it should be logged and reported in the dashboard.

findings.title: Monitor Critical CVEs Open
findings.description: Establish a monitoring system for the number of critical CVEs that are open to ensure platform security.
assumptions: The monitoring system will provide real-time updates.

evidence.excerpt: This guardrail metric monitors the number of critical CVEs that are open.

recommendations: Ensure alerts are sent to the security team immediately upon detection.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the monitoring system is in place WHEN a critical CVE is detected THEN the security team should be notified within 5 minutes.

findings.title: Conduct Experiment on CVE Remediation Impact
findings.description: Perform an experiment to measure the impact of remediating critical CVEs on platform stability and performance metrics.
assumptions: The experiment will yield actionable insights.

evidence.excerpt: Conduct an experiment to measure the impact of remediating critical CVEs on platform stability.

recommendations: Define success metrics for the experiment.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the experiment is conducted WHEN CVEs are remediated THEN the impact on stability and performance should be measured and reported.

findings.title: Evaluate Multi-Region Caching Solutions
findings.description: Assess and implement a multi-region caching solution to replace the single-region Redis cluster, addressing latency issues for global customers.
assumptions: Multi-region solutions will improve performance for all users.

evidence.excerpt: Need to migrate to Redis 7+ with multi-region replication.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the new caching solution is implemented WHEN users access the cache THEN latency should be reduced to under 100ms for all regions.

summary: The analysis has identified critical migration and upgrade requirements for the DataPipe platform, focusing on deprecated technologies, vendor lock-in risks, and the need for enhanced monitoring and management of dependencies. Each requirement is structured with clear acceptance criteria and dependencies, ensuring a comprehensive approach to addressing the identified risks.
